instruction: Implement a SwiftUI chart with Audio Graphs for visually impaired users, providing both visual and audio feedback for data trends.
Create a custom VoiceOver rotor in SwiftUI that allows users to quickly navigate between error messages in a form.
Demonstrate scalable UI in SwiftUI using .dynamicTypeSize() to support all Dynamic Type settings, including accessibility sizes.
Build a custom SwiftUI control with full accessibility support, including custom actions and accessibility values.
Show how to use accessibility modifiers to make a custom SwiftUI chart accessible, including custom labels and hints for each data point.
Implement a SwiftUI list with custom accessibility actions for VoiceOver users to mark items as favorites.
Create a SwiftUI view that uses accessibilityFocus() to programmatically move VoiceOver focus to an error message after a failed form submission.
Demonstrate how to use accessibilityCustomContent to provide additional context for a SwiftUI view representing a complex data object.
Show how to use accessibilityChildren to group related SwiftUI elements for a more logical VoiceOver navigation order.
Implement a SwiftUI chart that supports both Dynamic Type and VoiceOver, ensuring all data points are accessible and scalable.
